name: LLMWare
tagline: Unified framework for building local, private, knowledge-based LLM applications
seoTitle: LLMWare: Local RAG Framework for Knowledge-Based LLM Apps
metaDescription: LLMWare is an open-source Python framework that pairs a 300+ model catalog with a full RAG pipeline to build private, local, knowledge-based LLM applications.
overview: LLMWare is a Python framework for building knowledge-based LLM applications that run locally, privately, and securely. It is optimized for AI PCs, laptops, edge devices, and self-hosted deployment across Windows, Mac, and Linux, with support for inference backends like GGUF, OpenVINO, ONNX Runtime, and PyTorch.

The framework has two main parts. The first is a model catalog with 300+ prepackaged models, including 50+ small, RAG-optimized models from LLMWare's own SLIM, BLING, DRAGON, and Industry-BERT families, plus access to cloud models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. The second is an integrated RAG pipeline that handles parsing, text chunking, embedding, and querying documents so you can connect your own knowledge sources to a model.
features: Model catalog of 300+ models accessed through one consistent interface, covering GGUF, OpenVINO, ONNX Runtime, Hugging Face, Sentence Transformers, and major API-based models
50+ small (1-7B parameter) RAG-optimized models (BLING, DRAGON, SLIM, Industry-BERT) designed to run locally with provided context
Universal ingestion that parses and text-chunks mixed file types including PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, CSV, MD, JSON, HTML, and images
Library construct that organizes a knowledge base and lets you install and mix multiple embedding models and vector databases such as Milvus and ChromaDB
Flexible querying with text, semantic, hybrid, and metadata filters against an indexed library
Prompt-with-sources workflow that combines retrieval and inference, with post-inference fact-checking against the source evidence

gettingStarted.intro: LLMWare is installed from PyPI and runs on Python 3.10 through 3.14. Most use cases work with the minimal base install; a larger install adds commonly used optional libraries.

steps.heading: Install LLMWare
steps.body: Install the base package, or use the full install to pull in commonly used optional dependencies.
steps.code:
pip3 install llmware
# or, for the larger install with extra libraries:
pip3 install 'llmware[full]'

steps.heading: Load a model and run inference
steps.body: Every model is accessed through the ModelCatalog. Load a small, RAG-optimized model and run an inference with your own context.
steps.code:
from llmware.models import ModelCatalog

my_model = ModelCatalog().load_model("llmware/bling-phi-3-gguf")
output = my_model.inference("what is the future of AI?", add_context="Here is the article to read")

steps.heading: Build a knowledge library
steps.body: Create a library, add a folder of mixed document types (they are parsed, chunked, and indexed automatically), then install an embedding model with a vector database.
steps.code:
from llmware.library import Library

lib = Library().create_new_library("my_library")
lib.add_files("/folder/path/to/my/files")
lib.install_new_embedding(embedding_model_name="mini-lm-sbert", vector_db="milvus", batch_size=500)

steps.heading: Prompt with sources
steps.body: Combine retrieval and inference: load a model into a Prompt, attach a source document filtered by a query, then run the prompt against your knowledge.
steps.code:
from llmware.prompts import Prompt

prompter = Prompt().load_model("llmware/bling-tiny-llama-v0")
source = prompter.add_source_document("/folder/to/one/doc/", "filename", query="fast query")
responses = prompter.prompt_with_source("my query")

useCases: Building private, on-device RAG applications over internal documents such as contracts, invoices, and policies without sending data to the cloud
Running small, RAG-optimized models locally on a laptop or edge device to keep inference fast and cost-effective
Creating scalable enterprise knowledge bases that index mixed document types and support semantic and hybrid search
Automating enterprise tasks by combining knowledge retrieval with LLM inference and post-inference fact-checking against the source evidence
